<div id="main" class="col-md-9" role="main">

# YESCDS

<div class="section level1">

Cancer Data Science resources for DFCI YES for CURE – intended for use
by secondary and undergraduate students

<div class="figure">

![](reference/figures/YESCDS_map.png)

Use the ‘Articles’ tab above for detailed contents.

</div>

Contributors:

-   Alexandru Mahmoud, Channing Division of Network Medicine, Mass
    General Brigham
-   Brittany Michel, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
-   Latrice Landry, Perelman School of Medicine, University of
    Pennsylvania
-   Karen Burns White, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
-   Vincent Carey, Channing Division of Network Medicine, Mass General
    Brigham

This project supported in part by

-   NSF ACCESS Allocation BIR190004 “Engineering and disseminating a
    software and analysis ecosystem for genomic data science”

-   Chan-Zuckerberg Initiative EOSS Diversity & Inclusiveness
    EOSS-DI-0000000033 “Bioconductor: Sustaining a Worldwide Community
    of Genome Data Scientists”

-   National Cancer Institute 5U24CA180996-08 “Cancer Genomics:
    Integrative and Scalable Solutions in R/Bioconductor”

-   National Human Genome Research Institute 2U24HG004059-17
    “Bioconductor: An Open-Source, Open-Development Computing Resource
    for Genomics”

</div>

</div>

<div id="main" class="col-md-9" role="main">

# Package index

<div class="section level2">

## All functions

</div>

<div class="section level2">

-   `MA_cancer_rate_table()` : simple tables of confidence intervals for
    cancer incidence
-   `SEER_2018` : a data.frame with SEER estimates of cancer incidence
    for 2018
-   `brcamutSE` : a SummarizedExperiment collecting BRCA mutations and
    colData from curatedTCGAData
-   `build_deck()` : make a deck of cards with 52 elements and unicode
    symbols for suits
-   `build_simple_survival_curve()` : utility for survival curve
    illustration
-   `build_surv_for_mut()` : use curatedTCGAData to produce survival
    time structure and mutation matrix for a TCGA tumor type
-   `canada_crude` : a data.frame with (rounded) crude rates of cancer
    as presented at https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/dai/btd/asr
-   `cancer_map_app()` : use shiny for a simple cancer map, by type, for
    US
-   `cancer_map_usa()` : simple function for site-specific map
-   `cancer_map_world()` : simple map of cancer rates world wide
-   `compare_tumors()` : compare multiple tumor times with respect to
    survival
-   `do_new_surv()` : utility for survival curve, alternate shape
-   `faces()` : get faces of a collection of cards
-   `filter_woncan()` : generate site-specific rate table
-   `full_house()` : test for full house
-   `get_hcc_spatial_path()` : retrieve or cache SingleCellExperiment
    data for spatial transcriptomics of HCC
-   `is_flush()` : test for flush
-   `make_comparison()` : make an interactive histogram of rate ratios,
    or a scatterplot for rate comparisons, between two areas
-   `make_hist()` : make an interactive histogram for a given cancer
    site, based on a rate table
-   `make_pair()` : make interactive beeswarm or box plots for all
    cancer incidence rates in two areas
-   `map_app()` : use shiny to explore aspects of geographic mapping
-   `mass_cancer_map()` : map cancer rates for selected sites to MA
    counties
-   `mass_map()` : simple function to display HTML map of MA
-   `metabric` : a data.frame with clinical features of a breast cancer
    study (METABRIC) derived from cBioPortal
-   `one_pair()` : test for one pair
-   `ordered_seg_cal()` : big display of ordered CIs for California Lung
    and Bronchus incidence 2014-2018
-   `plot_seer_trend()` : plot the rate statistics over time for
    selected cancer sites and subgroups
-   `plotwci()` : simple display of confidence intervals for cancer
    incidence
-   `prostate_met_adeno` : a data.frame with clinical features of a
    metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma study
-   `show_5y_estimate()` : utility for survival curve illustration: 5y
    survprob estimate sketch
-   `show_median_estimate()` : utility for survival curve illustration:
    median estimate sketch
-   `statcrunch_msa` : geographic information from statcrunch.com on
    metropolitan statistical areas
-   `suits()` : get suits of a collection of cards
-   `table_woncan()` : simplify table view of WONDER CDC statistsics
-   `two_pairs()` : test for two pairs
-   `url_1R()` : give URL for responder example
-   `url_6NR()` : give URL for non-responder example
-   `us_county_geo` : locations of US counties
-   `us_map()` : simple function to display HTML map of US
-   `variation_app()` : simple app to show different ways of examining
    variation over body sites and MSAs
-   `vjc_cancer_net` : a table of data of Vince's contacts who developed
    cancer
-   `woncan` : cancer rate data by metropolitan statistical area,
    1999-2018, from CDC WONDER system
-   `woncan_meta` : metadata on cancer rate data by metropolitan
    statistical area, 1999-2018, from CDC WONDER system
-   `woncan_types()` : generate vector of cancer sites in CDC Wonder
    table
-   `world_cancer_2020_all` : a table of cancer rate data by country,
    retrieved from https://gco.iarc.fr/today/online-analysis-map
-   `world_geo_sf` : a simple features (sf) instance with boundaries and
    centroids for 241 countries or other political geographic entities

</div>

</div>

<div id="main" class="col-md-9" role="main">

# Articles

<div class="section">

### All vignettes

<div class="section-desc">

</div>

-   [A0 Introductory
    Notebook](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A0_intro_notebook.md):
-   [A1 Cancers in my
    world](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A1_cancer_table.md):
-   [A2 Cancer rates for public health
    assessment](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A2_rates.md):
-   [A3 Monitoring of cancer by public health
    institutions](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A3_monitoring.md):
-   [A4 Using interactive
    tables](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A4_interactive_tables.md):
-   [A5 Data
    graphics](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A5_data_graphics.md):
-   [A6 Age standardization of cancer
    rates](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/A6_standardization.md):
-   [B1 Cancer across the
    globe](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/B1_global.md):
-   [B2 Enhancing tables with
    geography](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/B2_table_plus_geo.md):
-   [B3 Cancer in our
    counties](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/B3_counties.md):
-   [C1 Cancer in our bodies: Overall
    view](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/C1_body_overall.md):
-   [C2 Variation in survival with
    cancer](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/C2_variation_survival.md):
-   [C3 Molecular origins of diverse
    cancers](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/C3_subtypes_molecular.md):
-   [D1 Clinical trials
    concepts](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/D1_clin_trial_concepts.md):
-   [D2 Equipoise: when medical knowledge can only advance by
    experimenting](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/D2_equipoise.md):
-   [D3 Randomization to reduce
    bias](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/D3_randomization_bias.md):
-   [D4 Methods of comparing
    treatments](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/D4_comparing_trt.md):
-   [E1 Inclusive genomic data science education, illustrated with
    cancer
    genomics](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/E1_heidelberg2022.md):
-   [E2: Language analysis and production with R and some
    LLMs](https://github.com/vjcitn/YESCDS/articles/E2_YESAI_2024.md):

</div>

</div>
